mission.title: Install and Configure Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
mission.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and operations of Kubernetes clusters in Azure. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution that integrates seamlessly with Azure services. Key trade-offs include reliance on Azure's infrastructure and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install Azure CLI
steps.description:
The Azure CLI is required to interact with Azure resources and manage your AKS cluster.

```bash

curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash

```

> This command installs the Azure CLI on Debian-based systems.

steps.title: Create AKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates an AKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version, node count, and region.

```bash

az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

```

> Replace 'myResourceGroup' and 'myAKSCluster' with your desired names.
commands: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus

steps.title: Configure kubectl
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the newly created AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster

```

> This command merges the AKS cluster credentials into your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks if the nodes in your AKS cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up monitoring and autoscaling for your AKS cluster.

```bash

az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

az aks update --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --enable-cluster-autoscaler --min-count 1 --max-count 5

```

> Enables monitoring for the AKS cluster.
> Enables cluster autoscaler.
commands: az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

uninstall.title: Delete AKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the AKS cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

az aks delete --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --yes --no-wait

```

> This will remove the cluster and all resources associated with it.

uninstall.title: Remove Resource Group
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the resource group, which will also delete all resources within it.

```bash

az group delete --name myResourceGroup --yes --no-wait

```

> Be cautious as this will permanently delete all resources in the resource group.

upgrade.title: Upgrade AKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your workloads and configurations.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup.yaml

az aks upgrade --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources in the cluster.
> Upgrade the AKS cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that the nodes are updated and in 'Ready' status.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to get AKS credentials'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified resource group or AKS cluster name may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the resource group and cluster name using 'az aks list'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient quota to fulfill request'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Azure subscription may have reached its resource quota.

**Fix:**
Check your Azure subscription quotas and request an increase if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' returns 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The AKS cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the AKS cluster using 'az aks show --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the AKS cluster may be down.

**Fix:**
Re-run 'az aks get-credentials --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster' to update kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Azure regions and improved integration with Azure Monitor.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.28.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure that all workloads are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: az aks create --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --node-count 3 --kubernetes-version 1.31 --location eastus
az aks enable-addons --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myAKSCluster --addons monitoring

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Azure CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
